prompt: I want to monitor Twitter posts that mention my competitor brand and collect the comments for downstream sentiment analysis.
expected_output: Route to Twitter/X and recommend the combined Twitter Advanced Search Comments Scraper workflow.
assertions: Routes to brand-mention-monitoring or comment-monitoring with Twitter/X as the primary platform
Recognizes that sentiment analysis is downstream and not a template capability
Recommends Twitter Advanced Search Comments Scraper as the primary template
Does not require a separate Twitter Advanced Search Scraper chain first

prompt: We already have a list of TikTok profile URLs and want to monitor competitor account activity.
expected_output: Route to competitor-account-monitoring and recommend TikTok Profile Scraper.
assertions: Routes to competitor-account-monitoring
Recognizes direct profile URLs are already available
Recommends TikTok Profile Scraper
Does not default to a search template

prompt: Find Reddit posts mentioning Brand X and collect comments from the most relevant threads.
expected_output: Recommend Reddit Search Scraper plus Reddit Post & Comments Scraper, with chain validation if presented as a workflow.
assertions: Routes to brand-mention-monitoring with comment-monitoring as part of the workflow
Uses Reddit as the primary platform
Recommends Reddit Search Scraper and Reddit Post & Comments Scraper
Treats sentiment analysis as downstream to collection

prompt: Monitor a competitor YouTube channel and collect comments from its new videos.
expected_output: Recommend YouTube Channel Scraper and YouTube Comments & Replies Scraper.
assertions: Routes to competitor-account-monitoring
Uses YouTube as the primary platform
Recommends YouTube Channel Scraper
Includes YouTube Comments & Replies Scraper when comments are needed

prompt: We only need Korean blog posts that mention the competitor name.
expected_output: Use the secondary shortlist and recommend Naver Blog SERP Scraper.
assertions: Routes to brand-mention-monitoring
Detects that a secondary regional platform is required
Recommends Naver Blog SERP Scraper
Does not default to a core global platform

prompt: We already have Twitter/X post URLs and only want replies and comments.
assertions: Routes to comment-monitoring
Recognizes direct post URLs are available
Recommends Twitter (X) Comments Scraper
Does not recommend discovery templates first

prompt: I want end-to-end competitor monitoring on Twitter/X: monitor competitor accounts, discover posts that mention the brand, and collect comments for downstream sentiment analysis.
expected_output: Support a full-chain recommendation across competitor-account-monitoring, brand-mention-monitoring, and comment-monitoring for Twitter/X.
assertions: Recognizes this as a full-chain request
Keeps Twitter/X as the primary platform
Includes account monitoring, mention monitoring, and comment monitoring in the recommendation
States that sentiment analysis is downstream to collection

prompt: We need competitor monitoring across all main social media platforms: Twitter/X, TikTok, Reddit, and YouTube.
expected_output: Support an all-core-platforms recommendation and organize the answer by platform.
assertions: Recognizes this as an all-core-platforms request
Covers Twitter/X, TikTok, Reddit, and YouTube
Organizes recommendations by platform or clearly grouped structure
Does not collapse everything into one unstructured mixed list

prompt: We want to monitor competitor-owned YouTube and TikTok accounts, plus collect comments from the most relevant videos afterward.
expected_output: Recognize this as a multi-track request rather than a single-track one.
assertions: Routes to multi-track rather than single-track
Uses competitor-account-monitoring as one part of the answer
Adds comment-monitoring because comments are explicitly requested
Keeps the recommendation centered on YouTube and TikTok

prompt: Find Reddit and YouTube posts that mention Brand X, then collect comments so our AI can analyze complaints and feedback later.
expected_output: Recommend brand-mention monitoring plus comment collection, and clearly say the AI analysis happens after collection.
assertions: Uses brand-mention-monitoring as a primary component
Adds comment-monitoring because comments are needed
Keeps Reddit and YouTube as the chosen platforms
Clearly separates data collection from later AI analysis
